description_long: This helicopter features a compact, robust fuselage painted primarily in a deep red color with a blue nose cone and engine cowl. The body is adorned with multiple engine exhaust ports on both sides, and the wings are equipped with several cylindrical attachments, likely representing armaments or fuel tanks. The cockpit is topped with a single, rounded, light blue-tinted window, providing visibility for the pilot. The tail section is short and thick, with a vertical stabilizer and a small horizontal stabilizer, and a tail rotor mounted at the end. The landing gear consists of two sturdy wheels beneath the fuselage, suggesting a design optimized for urban or rugged environments. The overall design is stylized, with exaggerated proportions and a somewhat cartoonish appearance.
description: A compact, red and blue helicopter with a rounded cockpit window, side exhausts, wing-mounted attachments, and robust landing gear. The design is stylized and features a prominent tail rotor.
description_view_0: Front view shows the blue nose cone, propeller, rounded cockpit window, and symmetrical wing-mounted attachments.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the red fuselage, blue engine cowl, multiple exhaust ports, and the landing gear beneath the body.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the tail section, vertical stabilizer, and the full span of the wings with their attachments.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the blue nose, exhaust ports, landing gear, and tail rotor.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four main rotor blades, straight and moderately wide, likely made of metal or composite material, colored gray with no visible markings.
attribute_tail_rotor: Tail rotor is present, featuring four short blades arranged in a cross pattern, colored gray, mounted at the end of the tail boom.
attribute_landing_skids: No traditional skids; instead, the helicopter uses two robust wheels as landing gear, attached to the underside of the fuselage.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Single, large, rounded cockpit window with a light blue tint, framed by the red fuselage, providing forward and upward visibility.
